---
# Tag frequency from a JSON string array by explode-and-count

**Dataset**: `gx_04_raw_json`

Computes a frequency distribution over the values inside a JSON `$.tags` array — the foundational hunt primitive for "what labels appear and how often" before drilling into anomalies. It uses `json_extract_scalar_array` to lift the array out of the raw `payload`, `arrayexpand` to fan it into one row per tag, then `comp count() ... by` the exploded column. Gotcha embodied: counting elements inside an array requires `arrayexpand` first — aggregating on the array field itself would count rows, not elements; use `json_extract_scalar_array` (not `json_extract_array`) when you want plain string elements to group on cleanly.

```sql
dataset = gx_04_raw_json
| alter tags_arr = json_extract_scalar_array(payload, "$.tags")
| arrayexpand tags_arr
| comp count() as cnt by tags_arr
| sort desc cnt
```
